verbs.altkv: Given fields with values of the form a,b,c,d,e,f emits a=b,c=d,e=f pairs.

verbs.bootstrap: Emits an n-sample, with replacement, of the input records. See also mlr sample and mlr shuffle.
verbs.case: Uppercases strings in record keys and/or values.
verbs.cat: Passes input records directly to output. Most useful for format conversion.
verbs.check: Consumes records without printing any output, Useful for doing a well-formatted check on input da...

verbs.count-distinct: Prints number of records having distinct values for specified field names. Same as uniq -c.

verbs.count: Prints number of records, optionally grouped by distinct values for specified field names.
verbs.cut: Passes through input records with specified fields included/excluded.
verbs.decimate: Passes through one of every n records, optionally by category.

verbs.fill-empty: Fills empty-string fields with specified fill-value.

verbs.fraction: For each record's value in specified fields, computes the ratio of that value to the sum of value...
verbs.gap: Emits an empty record every n records, or when certain values change.
verbs.grep: Passes through records which match the regular expression.
verbs.group-by: Outputs records in batches having identical values at specified field names.Options:
verbs.group-like: Outputs records in batches having identical field names.
verbs.gsub: Replaces old string with new string in specified field(s), with regex support for the old string ...
verbs.having-fields: Conditionally passes through records depending on each record's field names.

verbs.hex: Simple hex-dump. If zero file names are supplied, standard input is read.
verbs.histogram: Just a histogram. Input values < lo or > hi are not counted.
verbs.join: Joins records from specified left file name with records from all file names at the end of the Mi...
verbs.json-parse: Tries to convert string field values to parsed JSON, e.g. "[1,2,3]" -> [1,2,3].
verbs.json-stringify: Produces string field values from field-value data, e.g. [1,2,3] -> "[1,2,3]".

verbs.least-frequent: Shows the least frequently occurring distinct values for specified field names. The first entry i...
verbs.lecat: Simply echoes input, but flags CR characters in red and LF characters in green. If zero file name...
verbs.merge-fields: Computes univariate statistics for each input record, accumulated across specified fields.
verbs.most-frequent: Shows the most frequently occurring distinct values for specified field names. The first entry is...
verbs.nest: Explodes specified field values into separate fields/records, or reverses this.
verbs.nothing: Drops all input records. Useful for testing, or after tee/print/etc. have produced other output.

verbs.regtest: If no directories/files are specified, the directory ./test/cases is used by default. Recursively...
verbs.regularize: Outputs records sorted lexically ascending by keys.
verbs.remove-empty-columns: Omits fields which are empty on every input row. Non-streaming.
verbs.reorder: Moves specified names to start of record, or end of record.
verbs.repeat: Copies input records to output records multiple times.

verbs.sort-within-records: Outputs records sorted lexically ascending by keys.

verbs.sparsify: Unsets fields for which the key is the empty string (or, optionally, another specified value). On...

verbs.step: Computes values dependent on earlier/later records, optionally grouped by category.

verbs.summary: Show summary statistics about the input data.
verbs.tac: Prints records in reverse order from the order in which they were encountered.
